strict_unit_definition: A unique named organization with public evidence that its operating system learns or improves through a documented feedback, evaluation, memory, experimentation, decision, failure-learning, specialized-agent, workflow-adaptation, knowledge-curation, or governance loop. A mere AI deployment, tool listing, hypothetical use case, theory paper, or vendor claim without an organization-level improvement loop does not qualify.
counting_rule: Strict counts include only entries individually shown to meet strict_unit_definition. Zero means no qualifying entry was proven from the retrieved public evidence; it does not assert that none exists behind an inaccessible interface or after a future full audit.

largest_claim.reason: No exhaustive public-corpus universe or row-level like-for-like audit has been established. Large adjacent collections use different units, and the closest repository does not expose organization case records.

retrieval_environment.exa_search: blocked: free MCP rate limit returned HTTP 429
fallbacks: GitHub public API via authenticated gh CLI

collections.name: Evidently ML and LLM system design case-study database
collections.scope: Practical ML, LLM, and generative-AI product/application case studies from 150+ companies.
collections.raw_count_method: publisher-stated database composition count in the linked 2025-12-22 analysis; the rounded landing-page headline says 800
collections.strict_count_method: Landing-page scope and schema audit only; the 805 rows are application/system designs, not organization-level learning-loop records, and no row-level strict audit was available in the retrieved page.
exclusions: ML/LLM product implementations without an organizational improvement loop
multiple use cases from one company

audit.selection_method: Landing-page scope/schema review; no individual records were counted as audited units.

retrieval_evidence.method: Jina Reader public page and linked publisher analysis
retrieval_evidence.evidence: The landing page states 800 case studies from 150+ companies; the linked 2025-12-22 database-composition analysis gives the unrounded total as 805 use cases from 185 companies.

collections.name: Enterprise AI Case Studies
collections.scope: Human-curated reference library of documented enterprise AI deployments with architecture, implementation, evaluation, and citations.
collections.raw_count_method: publisher-stated exact count on landing page, cross-checked against 67 case links in the retrieved rendering
collections.strict_count_method: Landing-page title/summary scope screen only. The records document AI use cases or operational systems, but a stable row-decision audit was not captured and no organization was therefore counted.
exclusions: self-healing technical infrastructure without evidence the organization itself learns

audit.selection_method: All landing-page titles and summaries were screened at index level, but no stable row-decision ledger was captured; conservatively classified as scope-only.

retrieval_evidence.method: Jina Reader public landing and about pages
retrieval_evidence.evidence: Landing page states 67 Published and 8 Categories. About page specifies named organizations, production systems/pilots, verifiable metrics, and a five-document structure; individual pages carry Updated dates but the collection has no stated global update date.

collections.name: Case Ledger
collections.scope: Claimed searchable AI use-case and ROI ledger spanning industries, functions, workflows, maturity, sources, and failure variants.
collections.raw_count_method: publisher-stated exact homepage counter; not independently enumerable from the retrieved public page
collections.strict_count_method: Homepage and example-record audit only. The public evidence did not expose an enumerable row set or prove any record whose unit is a named self-learning organization.
exclusions: ROI records lacking a named organization-level learning loop

audit.selection_method: Homepage and example schema only; the claimed cases were not publicly enumerable.

retrieval_evidence.evidence: Homepage displayed Verified Cases 4,217, Source Docs 62k+, and an example CL-4188 with sector, baseline, ROI and sources; browse-row enumeration was not present in the retrieved rendering.

collections.name: CaseStudies.com
collections.scope: B2B vendor/customer success-story directory across software, healthcare, manufacturing, fintech, and other industries.
collections.raw_count_method: publisher-stated lower-bound counter (928,650+) on directory page; stored numeric value is the advertised floor
collections.strict_count_method: Directory-scope audit only. Its unit is a vendor/customer story and no strict topical filter or row-level organizational-loop audit was available.

audit.selection_method: Directory scope only; no topical enumeration or deterministic sample was available.

retrieval_evidence.method: public search result and directory rendering
retrieval_evidence.evidence: Directory advertised 43,957 B2B companies and 928,650+ validated case studies/customer stories; scope is software purchasing rather than self-learning organizations.

collections.name: Talented Learning LMS Case Study Directory
collections.scope: Vendor-published learning-system customer case studies categorized by solution, use case, client industry, vendor, functionality, and services.
collections.raw_count_method: publisher-stated lower bound ('over 100'); stored numeric value is the advertised floor
collections.strict_count_method: Directory-scope audit only. Employee/member training-system adoption is not evidence that an organization has a self-improving operating loop.

audit.selection_method: Directory scope only; no organization-loop row audit was performed.

retrieval_evidence.method: public search result and directory rendering
retrieval_evidence.evidence: Page says it assembled and categorized over 100 case studies from learning-system vendors and exposes vendor-oriented filters.

collections.name: ai-native-company-research
collections.scope: Public research notes on AI-native company operating systems, context engineering, internal agents, and self-improving loops.
collections.raw_count_unit: versioned content files excluding repository administration files
collections.raw_count_method: GitHub recursive tree: 24 blobs minus .gitignore, policy/contribution/license, and README = 19 content files
collections.strict_count_method: Complete filename/tree audit. The repository packages patterns, analyses, blueprints and source notes rather than one independently countable record per evidenced organization.
exclusions: source notes not normalized into organization case records

audit.selection_method: Complete repository tree/type audit established that organization cases are not its unit; individual files were therefore not treated as comparable audit units.

retrieval_evidence.method: GitHub public repository metadata and recursive tree API
retrieval_evidence.evidence: Repository default branch main had 24 blobs and was updated 2026-07-09T04:43:33Z; content paths comprise analyses, one blueprint, notes, patterns, and four source-note files.

collections.name: AI Trace
collections.scope: Free nonprofit transparency collection of cited, moderator-reviewed corporate AI practices, organized by named company.
collections.raw_count_method: publisher-displayed exact homepage counter; 1,602 practices and 4,528 cited sources were separate counters at retrieval
collections.strict_count_method: Deterministic audit of the six companies in the homepage Recently Updated list at retrieval. Company pages document AI use, while none of the sampled entries established an operating organizational improvement loop under the strict definition.
exclusions: AI adoption or automation without an improvement loop
data analysis without evidence that results feed an organizational learning cycle
unaudited companies outside the deterministic sample

audit.selection_method: All six company links in homepage Recently Updated order at the 2026-07-12 retrieval; this is a freshness sample, not a random or representative sample.

rows.label: PTC
rows.reason: Practices describe productivity, data-analysis, and customer-service AI use; no organization-level improvement loop is the evidenced unit.

rows.label: IBM
rows.reason: Practices describe corporate AI use; the sampled profile does not normalize evidence of an organizational learning loop.

rows.label: UMass Memorial Health
rows.reason: Healthcare AI practices are deployments and uses, not evidence of a feedback-driven organizational operating loop.

rows.label: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts
rows.reason: Productivity and data-analysis practices do not by themselves prove an organization-level improvement loop.

rows.label: Toast
rows.reason: Customer-service, productivity, and analysis uses are not sufficient evidence of organizational self-improvement.

rows.label: Oral-B
rows.reason: The profile's data-analysis practices do not establish a named organizational learning loop.

retrieval_evidence.evidence: Homepage displayed 278 companies tracked, 1,602 practices documented, 4,528 sources cited, 'Updated daily,' and six stable Recently Updated company links; no collection-wide last-update date was stated.
Methodology defines moderator review, a practice-level schema, at least one cited source per published practice, periodic re-checking, and public append-only edit history.

collections.name: World Bank AI Repository
collections.scope: Cross-sector public repository of implemented or active-pilot AI use cases relevant to development institutions and countries.
collections.raw_count_method: enumerable directory result counter ('Showing 1 - 15 of 111 results')
collections.strict_count_method: Deterministic first-five directory-order sample. The audited records describe AI solutions and decision-support tools, not evidence that a named organization's operating system learns through a documented loop.
exclusions: decision-support outputs without an organizational feedback cycle
global platforms without a single named operating organization as the evidence unit

audit.selection_method: First five records in the default public directory order on 2026-07-12; deterministic but not random or representative.

rows.label: Ibex: AI-Powered Pathology for Cancer Diagnosis
rows.reason: A deployed diagnostic product/workflow, not a named organization's improvement loop.

rows.label: FAO GAEZ v4
rows.reason: A modelling platform producing assessments; no organization-level learning cycle is documented as the unit.

rows.label: Kenya Agricultural Observatory Platform
rows.reason: A farmer decision-support system; the record does not evidence an operating feedback loop inside a named organization.

rows.label: CropWatch
rows.reason: A monitoring and assessment system, not evidence that an organization adapts its own operating system from the outputs.

rows.label: Virtual Agronomist
rows.reason: An ongoing advisory service; service personalization alone does not prove organizational learning.

retrieval_evidence.evidence: Directory reported 111 results, 15 per first page, exposed stable use-case URLs and filters, and showed 2026-04-16 update dates for the audited first five records; no repository-wide last-update date was stated.

collections.name: Lean Enterprise Institute organization case studies
collections.scope: Public case studies from organizations partnered with Lean Enterprise Institute, focused on lean operations, leadership, decision processes, capability development, and continuous improvement.
collections.raw_count_method: complete deterministic count of the eight case-study cards on the public page
collections.strict_count_method: Complete card-by-card audit. Included only unique named organizations whose card explicitly describes hoshin kanri strategy deployment or capability development as an operating improvement cycle; duplicate Logoplaste coverage counts once.
exclusions: workflow or outcome improvement without an explicit feedback/learning cycle
scholarship/internship reflection without an evidenced organization operating loop
duplicate case study for the same organization

audit.selection_method: All eight case-study cards in public page order on 2026-07-12.

rows.label: Respect for People: Making the Job Easier for Workers
rows.reason: The card describes workflow and outcome improvement at Turner Construction but does not itself document the feedback or learning cycle required for strict inclusion.

rows.label: Restaurant Partner Story
rows.reason: The operating improvement is relevant, but the organization is unnamed on the public index and therefore fails the unique named-organization rule.

rows.label: Addressing a Critical Problem in Strategic Planning and Execution
rows.reason: The card documents adoption and six-month learning from hoshin kanri strategy development and deployment.

rows.label: Ensuring Success of Hoshin Kanri
rows.reason: Qualifying Logoplaste evidence, but the organization is already counted by the preceding card.

rows.label: How Mercer Celgar Reinvented Its Strategic Planning Process
rows.reason: The card explicitly describes adapting leadership and team capability through hoshin kanri.

rows.label: How to Improve Decision Flow
rows.reason: The card documents a decision-flow mapping process at GE Appliances, but not an explicit feedback or learning cycle.

rows.label: James P. Womack Scholarship & Philanthropy Fund case study
rows.reason: The card reflects on internship outcomes but does not establish an operating improvement loop for a named host organization.

rows.label: Keeping Classroom Technologies Functioning
rows.reason: The card documents a lean-operations change and outcome at Trafera, but not the operating feedback or learning cycle required for strict inclusion.

retrieval_evidence.evidence: The page exposed eight cards and stable download URLs; two unique named organizations met the conservative strict rule, five cards lacked sufficient named-loop evidence, and one duplicated Logoplaste.
